workload_profile.reasoning: EKS-based microservices (backend API + security scanner), RDS PostgreSQL, Secrets Manager, IRSA. Multi-AZ for prod. GitOps-ready (ArgoCD). Moderate traffic, stateful, security-sensitive.

security.rationale: KMS, IRSA, private subnets, VPC Flow Logs, CloudTrail, S3 encryption. Placeholder secrets and db_password in state need remediation.
evidence_found: KMS keys for RDS and Secrets Manager
IRSA for ESO and LB controller
key_risks: RDS password flows through state via secrets.tf
recommended_actions: Use external secret mutation for DB password
Add CloudTrail bucket random_id for global uniqueness

reliability.rationale: Multi-AZ RDS for prod, 2 AZs, backup retention 7 days. EKS single node group.
key_risks: EKS single node group; no spread across AZs for node group
recommended_actions: Consider node group in multiple AZs for HA

cost_optimization.rationale: VPC endpoints (S3, ECR, Logs) reduce NAT cost. Cost tags present.

operational_excellence.rationale: Terraform CI with validate, plan. Backend CI: lint, test, security, SBOM, promote gate. CI pushes to GHCR, not ECR.
evidence_found: Backend ci.yml: lint, test, security, SBOM
key_risks: Platform provides ECR but backend pushes to GHCR; deployment path mismatch
recommended_actions: Add ECR push step to backend CI for AWS deployment

observability.rationale: VPC Flow Logs, CloudTrail, RDS CloudWatch logs, RDS CPU/connections alarms. Missing EKS cluster alarm.
key_risks: No EKS-specific alarm for node or control plane health
recommended_actions: Add EKS cluster alarm for control plane or node health

compliance_evidence_quality.rationale: Audit logging (CloudTrail, VPC Flow Logs). Compliance mapping not documented.

missing_components.reason: Placeholder secrets (REPLACE_WITH_*) in Secrets Manager; db_password flows through Terraform state

production_baseline.conditional_reason: CONDITIONAL: Placeholder secrets and db_password in state must be replaced before production. CI pushes to GHCR; add ECR push for AWS-native deployment. CloudTrail bucket name not globally unique.

findings.title: CloudTrail S3 bucket name not globally unique
findings.summary: S3 bucket names are globally unique; current pattern may collide across accounts/regions.
findings.evidence: Bucket uses ${var.project}-${var.environment}-cloudtrail-logs; no random_id suffix. Per terraform-deployment-checklist.md, S3/CloudTrail buckets need random_id for uniqueness.
findings.impact: terraform apply may fail with BucketAlreadyExists when deploying to new account or if another stack uses same project/env.
findings.recommendation: Add random_id resource and suffix to CloudTrail bucket name: ${var.project}-${var.environment}-cloudtrail-${random_id.suffix.hex}

steps: Update aws_s3_bucket.cloudtrail bucket attribute to include random_id.suffix.hex

findings.title: Placeholder secrets in Secrets Manager
findings.summary: Backend and security secrets contain REPLACE_WITH_* placeholders.
findings.evidence: secret_string includes 'REPLACE_WITH_OPENSSL_RAND_HEX_32_OUTPUT' and 'REPLACE_WITH_STRONG_API_KEY'. Observed in secrets.tf.
findings.impact: Production deployment will use insecure placeholder values; JWT signing and API key auth will be weak or predictable.
findings.recommendation: Replace placeholders before prod: use External Secrets mutation, or terraform apply with -var='db_password=...' and pre-populate Secrets Manager with real values via separate process.

steps: Store in Secrets Manager via AWS CLI or separate bootstrap; or use External Secrets with templateFrom
Remove placeholder values from terraform or use data source to reference externally-created secret
validation_steps: Verify secret values in AWS Console; ensure no REPLACE_WITH in prod

findings.title: RDS password in Terraform state
findings.summary: db_password flows through secrets.tf into state; deployment checklist requires secrets outside state.
findings.evidence: secrets.tf embeds var.db_password in aws_secretsmanager_secret_version.backend secret_string. RDS uses var.db_password directly. Both flow through Terraform state.
findings.impact: Terraform state contains sensitive credentials; state compromise exposes DB password. Compliance risk for regulated environments.
findings.recommendation: Use external secret creation: create RDS and Secrets Manager secret via bootstrap script or separate pipeline that does not store password in state. Or use SSM Parameter Store with data source; avoid passing password through Terraform.

steps: Document: db_password must be set via TF_VAR or -var at apply time; never commit
For prod: use AWS Secrets Manager rotation or bootstrap that creates secret outside Terraform
Consider: RDS IAM auth for app; reduce need for static password in state
validation_steps: Ensure no secrets in state file; use remote state with encryption

findings.title: No EKS cluster alarm
findings.summary: Observability has RDS alarms only; no EKS control plane or node health alarm.
findings.evidence: observability.tf defines rds_cpu and rds_connections alarms only. No aws_cloudwatch_metric_alarm for EKS.
findings.impact: EKS cluster or node issues may go undetected; slower incident response.
findings.recommendation: Add CloudWatch alarm for EKS cluster status or node group health (e.g., cluster endpoint 5xx or node CPU).

steps: Add aws_cloudwatch_metric_alarm for EKS cluster (e.g., ControlPlaneRequests or custom metric)
Or add alarm for EKS node group instance health if available

findings.title: Backend CI pushes to GHCR, not ECR
findings.summary: taskforge-backend CI pushes images to ghcr.io; platform-infra provides ECR repositories.
findings.evidence: push-and-sign job uses ghcr.io registry. Platform-infra outputs ecr_backend_url and ecr_push_policy_arn for ECR push.
findings.impact: ArgoCD/Kustomize overlays may reference ECR; image source mismatch. Manual sync of images or dual-push needed for AWS deployment.
findings.recommendation: Add ECR push step to backend CI when deploying to AWS; use ecr_push_policy_arn from platform-infra outputs. Support both GHCR (GitHub-native) and ECR (AWS) as deployment targets.

steps: Add job or step to backend CI: docker push to ECR using AWS credentials (OIDC or secrets)
Use platform-infra output ecr_backend_url as push target
validation_steps: CI pushes to ECR; ArgoCD pulls from ECR

findings.title: CloudTrail depends_on should include encryption config
findings.summary: Apply-order doc requires bucket policy before CloudTrail; encryption config should complete before trail creation for consistency.
findings.evidence: CloudTrail depends_on lists bucket, policy, public_access_block. aws_s3_bucket_server_side_encryption_configuration exists but is not in depends_on. Terraform infers from bucket reference; explicit inclusion improves clarity.
findings.impact: Low; Terraform typically orders correctly. Explicit depends_on improves apply-order validation and documentation.
findings.recommendation: Add aws_s3_bucket_server_side_encryption_configuration.cloudtrail to CloudTrail depends_on for consistency with apply-order best practices.

remediation_summary.ordering_reasoning: F2 (placeholder secrets) is deployment_blocker — must fix before prod. F3 (secrets in state) is security improvement. F1 (CloudTrail uniqueness) prevents apply failures. F4–F6 are observability and ops improvements.

diagram.content:
flowchart TB
  subgraph Client
    C[Client / ArgoCD]
  end

  subgraph EKS["EKS Cluster"]
    Backend[taskforge-backend
    FastAPI :8000]
    Security[taskforge-security
    CVE/Trivy :8081]
  end

  subgraph Data
    RDS[(RDS PostgreSQL)]
  end

  subgraph AWS_Services
    ECR[ECR Repos]
    SM[Secrets Manager]
    KMS[KMS]
  end

  subgraph Observability
    CW[CloudWatch Alarms]
    CT[CloudTrail]
    VF[VPC Flow Logs]
  end

  C -->|ALB/Ingress| Backend
  C -->|ALB/Ingress| Security
  Backend -->|SQLAlchemy| RDS
  Backend -->|IRSA/ESO| SM
  Security -->|API Key| SM
  EKS -->|Pull| ECR
  RDS -->|KMS| KMS
  SM -->|KMS| KMS
diagram.caption: Inferred TaskForge architecture: EKS hosts backend and security services; RDS, ECR, Secrets Manager; CloudTrail and VPC Flow Logs for audit.

deployment_frequency.evidence: Backend CI triggers on push to main; promote job has manual environment approval. Terraform CI on terraform/** changes.
deployment_frequency.recommendation: Add Prometheus pipeline metrics or ArgoCD sync metrics for actual deployment count.

lead_time.evidence: Pipeline stages: lint → test → security → sbom → docker → push-and-sign → promote. Manual gate before prod.
lead_time.recommendation: Track commit-to-deploy via GitHub API or Prometheus exporter.

change_failure_rate.recommendation: Track pipeline failures; integrate with incident system for deployment-caused incidents.

mttr.recommendation: Integrate PagerDuty/Opsgenie for alert-to-resolution tracking; add runbook linkage.
